query: Mac app with dynamic AI EQ complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: ‎djay - DJ App & AI Mixer App - App Store
results.content:
hard earned money. was a devout fan of this app and have subscribed to not just the app but to Tidal and Beatsource in addition. A fan until the issues over the past 6 to 8 months have really soured me and has me wondering if the developers haven’t given up on the app, too. SO MANY Jed glitches and problems. Trying to get technical assistance and having every email request go unanswered and ignored for the past 4 months is really frustrating. There are so many viable alternatives. Some that are even better because they’re stable. Stable in their technology and stable in their customer service. It’s easy to sell a product and collect money each month but it’s something altogether different to have the integrity to stand behind that product and stand out from the competition. [...] Tracks that freeze in the middle, won’t play at all, tracks that won’t stay in sync, or they drag, a lot of times only half the song loads, won’t load at all, or will take a full 3 1/2 minutes just to load, ON WI-FI. I even purchased a brand new 2014 iPhone Plus and a 9th Generation iPad to trouble shoot and remove the possibility of it being an issue with the end user tech. To my disbelief, things got even worse. This app’s glory days have long passed and there’s nobody available to assist you with technical issues or answer your questions. The Help email option, for all accounts and purposes, should be taken down from the website. That department has been dismantled, apparently. No reason to waste your time or spend your hard earned money. was a devout fan of this app and have [...] Developer Response

  Hi soopmeister, Thanks for your feedback and we appreciate you bringing this issue to our attention. We will look into this further. If there's any other information you'd like to share about the issue, please contact us at feedback@algoriddim.com so we may take a closer look. Kind regards, Algoriddim Support
 ### Djay Pro AI is good, not great yet

  Teckmarkram

results.title: This FREE App Fixes macOS Audio (And It’s Open Source)
results.content:
46 comments
### Transcript:
Welcome to this brief guide on how 
to manage audio devices on Mac OS. Mac OS audio management has 
always been a little bit limited. If you've ever wanted to turn down a loud 
browser game without silencing your music. Or send your Spotify playlist 
to your Bluetooth speakers while keeping your system alerts in your 
headphones, you know the frustration. Usually these features are locked behind a 
paywall in apps like SoundSource or eqMac. But today we're looking at FineTune, 
a completely free, open source menu bar app that brings professional grade 
audio routing and control to your Mac. To get started, we're heading over to the 
official GitHub repository for FineTune. Because this is FOSS, or free [...] like a native part of your Mac. The most important toggle here is launch at login. You definitely want this turned on so that 
all of your custom EQs, microphone selections, and audio routings are restored automatically 
every time you restart your computer. You can also customize the app's aesthetic. Under the menu bar icon section, you 
can cycle through different styles. There's a standard speaker, a modern 
waveform, and my personal favorite, the vintage reel-to-reel recorder icon. Just remember that changing the icon 
does require a quick restart of the app. And while you're here, don't forget 
to enable automatic update checking. That way you always have the latest 
version with all of the greatest features. Finally, if you've been experimenting with routing [...] and things have gotten a bit too complicated, the reset all settings button at 
the bottom is your safety net. One click will wipe all of your 
custom configurations and return everything to the factory defaults, 
giving you a completely clean slate. And there you go. FineTune is a powerful, lightweight, 
and most importantly, free alternative to expensive audio controllers 
like SoundSource and eqMac. It bridges the gap between basic system 
volume and professional audio management. I've included the link to the GitHub repository in the description below so you can quickly 
download it and try it out for yourself. Happy listening, and I'll 
catch you in my next video.

results.title: New AI EQ from Waves...
results.content:
I have a hunch an awful lot of people won't get to the understanding part, and just try presets and some control tweaks and either like what they got or don't get what the fuss is about.

 I also checked out Silk Vocal at the same time, to see if it had advantages for vocals. But it seemed that it was just easier by virtual of less flexibility. Maybe I missed some nuance, but it didn't seem like much advantage of having it if you have and understand this one. [...] I should add that I've quit chasing plugins the past several years, and stick with some pretty basic functions. Yeah, I have all the iZotope stuff, etc., and it's very comprehensive, and sure, things like transient shaping are useful from time to time, if you're stuck with the tracks you have. But it seems at some point you get to the place where you have a zillion flavors of compressors and dynamic EQs, and instead you toss old Rvox on a vocal and, crap, that track sounds dam good. Image 58: Lol smiley

 But just to be clear, I do like this plugin a lot.

results.title: I tried 100 AI Music Tools… These are the ONLY ones worth using
results.content: and play your song it will analyze what's going on in your song and then add what it thinks it needs to have a professional sound it added this EQ here it added a stabilizer it added an impact or changed the dynamic impact of each individual frequency band a stereo imager Dynamic EQ and then most importantly the limiter itself now I will say that often times I find ozone to be a bit brighter than what I like to listen to so I like to turn these down I also like this case we don't have any bass that's why it's boosting this really low frequency it's just a piano and voice in this case so take this with the grain of salt it's also really good for identifying frequency spots with the dynamic EQ adding in some stereo width with the imager all of that is going to make your mix sound that much

results.title: 5 Miraculous Automatic EQ Plugins You Should Check Out
results.content:
The two main parameters, Tame and Recover, suppress and counteract masking between elements within the signal respectively, slanted one way or the other by the Bias control; while Brighten and Boost can be brought into play for dynamic high- and low/mid-frequency emphasis/deemphasis if required. Happily, metering is plentiful, so you can see at a glance how much taming, recovery and brightening is taking place; and the affected frequency range is easily adjustable, should you only want to fix up a particular part of the spectrum.

As long as you understand what it’s doing and handle those controls with care, the improvement that Gullfoss makes to any (generally well balanced) mix really can be transformative, with a natural cleanliness that never fails to amaze. [...] Production Expert

# 5 Miraculous Automatic EQ Plugins You Should Check Out

One of the most powerful mixing tools available to the 21st century producer, the automatic or ‘intelligent’ EQ is a radical variant on dynamic EQ that constantly pulls the full (or deliberately constrained) frequency spectrum of the incoming signal towards an internally established optimal target in real time by applying hundreds of dynamically responsive peak filters to it. It’s all you need to smooth out irksome resonances and general harshness, and here we’re taking in five of the leading options.

### oeksound Soothe 2 [...] Finally we have Baby Audio’s “intelligent signal balancer” Smooth Operator Pro, a more powerful take on the original Smooth Operator this FFT-based spectral compressor, like its predecessor, works by dynamically conforming the input signal to a user-defined response curve, making it just as useful for corrective clean-up as for more creative reshaping of tone. Operation is intuitive: drag the central node to set the overall compression threshold, then sculpt the response across the frequency spectrum with additional nodes. In this new version, those nodes can be freely added and removed, and each can run its own dynamic processing profile for more precise control. A sidechain input is available for cross-channel ducking, and the Focus slider adjusts filter sharpness. The big leap forward
